name: Chocolate Cherry Torte 01- Country Living
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare Dried Cherry Filling: Combine dried cherries, water, and 3 tablespoons granulated sugar in a saucepan. Bring to a boil over high heat. Reduce heat to low and simmer until cherries are soft and liquid is reduced by half. Remove from heat and blend the mixture into a chunky puree. Return to saucepan with remaining cherry mixture, add cornstarch, and stir well. Heat to boiling, stirring constantly. Cook until thickened. Stir in Marsala and lemon juice. Cool to room temperature.
2. Prepare Coffee Syrup: Combine coffee with 3 tablespoons granulated sugar and Marsala. Set aside.
3. Prepare Chocolate Cake: Dissolve cocoa in boiling water. Set aside to cool. Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease a 15½ by 10½-inch jelly-roll pan and line it with waxed paper. Beat sugar and shortening until fluffy. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Fold in vanilla and cocoa mixture. Sift together flour, salt, baking powder, and baking soda. Alternately add to the fat mixture with buttermilk, beating until smooth. Spread batter evenly in prepared pan and bake 18-20 minutes or until center springs back when gently pressed. Cool cake on a wire rack for 10 minutes. Invert onto wire rack, remove paper, and cool completely.
4. Prepare Cream-Cheese Filling: Beat cream cheese with confectioners' sugar until smooth. Beat cream until soft peaks form. Fold into cream-cheese mixture just until blended. Do not over-mix.
5. Assemble Torte: Cut chocolate cake into three 9 by 5-inch rectangles using a 9 by 5-inch loaf pan as a guide. Line the inside of the pan with plastic wrap, allowing it to extend over the sides. Place one cake rectangle in the bottom of the pan and brush generously with Coffee Syrup. Spread half of cheese filling on top. Add another cake rectangle, brush with Coffee Syrup, and spread cherry filling. Top with remaining cake and brush with remaining syrup. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate at least 4 hours or overnight.
6. Prepare Chocolate Meringue Twigs: Preheat oven to 275°F. Line two baking sheets with parchment paper. Mix sugar and egg whites in a bowl set over hot water, beating until thick and nearly double in volume. Remove from heat and continue beating until stiff peaks form. Sift cocoa powder into meringue and fold in. Pipe 3-4 inch long twigs onto the sheets. Bake for 1 1/2 hours or until firm. Cool on racks. Melt chocolate and dip fork in to create stripes. Refrigerate until hardened. Remove from paper and store airtight until ready to use.
7. Serve: Remove torte from pan, pulling up plastic wrap. Place on serving plate and gently remove plastic. Press chocolate twigs upright onto sides of torte. Tie ribbon if desired, ending in a bow. Top with fresh cherries and serve, or refrigerate up to 2 hours before serving.
